what_it_does: This file is a JSON catalog of reusable UI/design components extracted from multiple pitch-deck and landing-page substrates. Each entry names a component, describes its visual form and behavior, gives a reuse rule, and tags the originating source deck.
observations: Components are sourced from at least seven substrates: capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, product-vi-landing, product-vt, and recent-pitches.
Many component names appear more than once with different descriptions and constraints, e.g., StatCard, ArrowBulletList/Arrow Bullet List, PersonCard, NetworkGraphDiagram, and FeatureCard.
Entries include fine-grained visual specs: colors (gold, cream, warm grey, olive-gold), sizes, font sizes, border styles, radii, padding, and spacing.
Reuse notes often prescribe mandatory companions, counts, and contexts (e.g., 'always in pairs', 'one per slide maximum', 'never use more than two').
Some components are flagged as brand-critical or mandatory (V brand badge, DiagonalStripeBar, hexagon/diamond product badges).
The file is truncated near the end of the last entry, cutting off its reuse note.
There is no schema version, canonical ID, or cross-reference field; components are flat records.
standouts: Heavy duplication of component names across source decks with divergent specs.
Brand-identity-critical geometric badges (hexagon for Property Wizard, diamond for Vi) and a mandatory V-badge footer.
Semantic reuse rules that encode meaning into visual treatments (dashed = notional, hatch = conclusion, drop shadow = punchline).
conventions: Top-level JSON array of objects with keys name, description, reuse_note, _src.
Visual language consistently uses gold/cream/warm-grey palettes, rounded rectangles, dashed lines, and arrow markers.
Constraints are expressed as prescriptive rules (max counts, mandatory pairings, forbidden contexts).
Each component is attributed to a source substrate via _src.
concerns: Duplicate component names may represent unresolved merges rather than intentional variants.
Inconsistent naming style within the same file (space-separated vs. camelCase vs. hyphenated).
Divergent descriptions for the same name could confuse a consumer expecting a single design token.
File truncation leaves the final entry incomplete.
No explicit versioning, canonical identifiers, or conflict-resolution metadata.
notes: A source-tagged but not-yet-reconciled component inventory that captures recurring deck patterns with strong visual and semantic constraints.
questions: Are duplicate component names intended to be merged into one canonical component or kept as source-specific variants?
When two entries share a name but conflicting specs, which description governs?
What is the intended consumer of this file: design documentation, a code generator, a Figma plugin, or a review index?
How should the truncated final entry be completed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a reusable component catalog, but the file currently behaves like a concatenated dump of source-specific component notes with duplicates and divergent rules, not a unified design system.
apparent_themes: Atomic design-system thinking for presentation decks and landing pages.
Brand consistency enforced through color, geometry, and mandatory footer marks.
Constraint-driven reuse where visual treatment carries semantic meaning.
Preservation of visual grammar (arrows, hatch, dashed borders, pills, orbs).
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to extract and standardize every recurring visual pattern across decks so that future slides can reuse them consistently while preserving brand identity and rhetorical meaning.
novelty: The reuse notes encode design semantics (e.g., dashed borders signal notional grouping, hatch bands signal conclusion, drop shadows signal punchline) rather than merely describing appearance, turning the catalog into a lightweight semantic design vocabulary.
contribution: Provides a source-attributed, visually detailed inventory of candidate reusable components and their constraints, which a fused system can use as raw material for normalization and design-token extraction.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON catalog of reusable presentation and landing-page UI components extracted from multiple design substrates, each with visual description, reuse constraints, and source attribution.
intention_for_embedding: To collect and preserve recurring design components across pitch decks and landing pages so they can be standardized and reused consistently.
